# BODY — Infrastructure map for AI coding tools

**Audience:** Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Aider, and any future agent reading this repo. This file is the **source of truth** for how the codebase actually runs. Read this BEFORE editing actor signatures, expertise files, scraper templates, self-awareness components, or backend interfaces.

**Status:** Auto-maintained. Sections marked `<!-- AUTOGEN:start:name -->...<!-- AUTOGEN:end:name -->` are regenerated by `tools/regenerate_body.py` and verified by `tests/test_body_in_sync.py`. **Do not hand-edit the content inside those markers** — your changes will be overwritten on the next commit.

---

## Read this first

m3xa-core is a **didactic public reference** for the *House pattern* — a self-aware intelligence agent built on three layers:

1. **Ingestion** — six scraper templates feed a single LanceDB table (`unified_feed`) keyed on document id + vector
2. **Query pipeline** — a 7-actor sequence (classifier → router → assembler → agent_hub → retriever → synthesizer → evaluator) that picks 1-3 expertises out of a pool per query and answers from retrieved docs
3. **Self-awareness loop** — nine runtime components that watch the pipeline from the inside, propose soul amendments, surface drift, and feed labeled exchanges back into retrieval

It is **anonymized by design**. Real source names, server hostnames, Telegram bot tokens, and the proprietary editorial voice of the live system this descends from are not committed here. See `docs/source_naming.md` for the translation scheme.

If you're going to change the codebase, the things you MUST preserve:

1. **The 7-actor sequence is the contract.** Don't merge actors. Don't reorder. Adding an actor requires updating BODY.md + `tests/test_pipeline.py`.
2. **Router output schema is fixed.** Returns `{"expertises": [...], "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "rationale": "..."}`. Downstream actors depend on this shape. See `m3xa_core/schemas.py`.
3. **`unified_feed` is the only retrieval table.** Scrapers write to it; the retriever reads from it; no other vector stores. See `docs/SCHEMA.md`.
4. **Self-awareness components write to their own files, not to each other.** Each of the nine has a clear write path (an artifact JSON or markdown file). No cross-writes. The soul_amendment_engine reads from self_evaluator's output, doesn't reach into its memory.
5. **Vendor SDKs live inside `m3xa_core/backends/` only.** Don't import `anthropic` or `voyageai` from an actor or a scraper. The backend protocols are the swap surface.
6. **No real source names anywhere.** `tools/check_anonymization.py` enforces this; CI blocks the build on any leak.

---

## Architecture invariants

- **Library, not service.** No HTTP server, no daemon, no scheduler in the repo. Caller drives the pipeline (`Pipeline.run(query)`).
- **No state between queries — except by design.** The pipeline is functional. The self-awareness components write artifacts to disk; subsequent queries read those artifacts. State is explicit, not implicit.
- **One LLM call per actor (max).** Classifier → 1 Haiku. Router → 1 Haiku. Synthesizer → 1 Sonnet (or 2 with regen). Evaluator → 1 Haiku. Total max 5 LLM calls per query, plus whatever the agent_hub fires.
- **Scrapers are templates, not live integrations.** No cookies, no auth tokens, no specific selectors for real sites. Each template documents the *shape*; the reader fills in the rest for their target.

---

## Self-healing roadmap

| Layer | Status | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| **Spec** (BODY.md) | ✅ live | Declares what "working" looks like |
| **Drift detector** | 🟡 partial | `tests/test_body_in_sync.py` catches doc-vs-code drift at CI time; `tools/check_anonymization.py` catches real-name leaks |
| **Auto-heal actor** | 🔮 future | Future Actor 8 that detects missing expertise files, malformed router output, env-var gaps, scraper template misuse. Fails with repair instructions. |

## Actors

The 7-actor pipeline. Each runs in sequence; see `m3xa_core/pipeline.py` for the orchestrator.

<!-- AUTOGEN:start:actors -->
| Actor | File | First-line docstring |
|---|---|---|
| `agent_hub` | [`m3xa_core/actors/agent_hub.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/agent_hub.py) | Actor 3 — Agent Hub. Fires zero or more data agents in parallel. |
| `assembler` | [`m3xa_core/actors/assembler.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/assembler.py) | Actor 2 — Expertise Assembler. Concatenates picked expertises into a system prompt. |
| `classifier` | [`m3xa_core/actors/classifier.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/classifier.py) | Actor 1 — Classifier. Tags a query with topics, entities, intent. |
| `evaluator` | [`m3xa_core/actors/evaluator.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/evaluator.py) | Actor 7 — Evaluator. Scores the synthesizer output against a rubric. |
| `retriever` | [`m3xa_core/actors/retriever.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/retriever.py) | Actor 4 — Retriever. Vector search over unified_feed + entity boost reranking. |
| `router` | [`m3xa_core/actors/router.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/router.py) | Actor 1.5 — Expertise Router. Picks 1-3 expertises per query. |
| `synthesizer` | [`m3xa_core/actors/synthesizer.py`](./m3xa_core/actors/synthesizer.py) | Actor 5 — Synthesizer. Single Sonnet call producing the user-facing response. |
<!-- AUTOGEN:end:actors -->

## Self-awareness components

The nine runtime loops that make the pipeline self-aware. See `concepts/self_awareness_loop.md` for the design.

<!-- AUTOGEN:start:self_awareness -->
| Component | File | First-line docstring |
|---|---|---|
| `auto_healer` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/auto_healer.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/auto_healer.py) | auto_healer — remediates RED items with known fixes, without asking. |
| `claude_interaction_log` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/claude_interaction_log.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/claude_interaction_log.py) | claude_interaction_log — persistent session memory for coding-agent sessions. |
| `conversation_learner` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/conversation_learner.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/conversation_learner.py) | conversation_learner — aggregates follow-up + intent stats across sessions. |
| `health_caveats_writer` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/health_caveats_writer.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/health_caveats_writer.py) | health_caveats_writer — temporary "the X scraper is degraded" warnings injected into RAG. |
| `lessons_indexer` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/lessons_indexer.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/lessons_indexer.py) | lessons_indexer — LESSONS.md -> tagged JSON for runtime retrieval. |
| `log_manager` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/log_manager.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/log_manager.py) | log_manager — rotation + compaction + archival. |
| `proactive_intel` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/proactive_intel.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/proactive_intel.py) | proactive_intel — five triggers that initiate output without a user query. |
| `self_evaluator` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/self_evaluator.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/self_evaluator.py) | self_evaluator — six-check structural audit per response. |
| `soul_amendment_engine` | [`m3xa_core/self_awareness/soul_amendment_engine.py`](./m3xa_core/self_awareness/soul_amendment_engine.py) | soul_amendment_engine — patterns become proposed soul edits, gated by #approve. |
<!-- AUTOGEN:end:self_awareness -->

## How to use BODY.md as an AI agent

1. **First read** — get the architecture invariants + read this file's "Read this first" section.
2. **For any task** — check the relevant AUTOGEN section. If you're adding a scraper, scan the scraper templates table. If you're adding a dependency, find its row in the stack table.
3. **If you change code that affects an AUTOGEN section** — run `python tools/regenerate_body.py` before committing. The pre-commit hook also runs it. CI fails if BODY.md is out of sync.
4. **If you change something fundamental** (a new actor, removing a self-awareness component, changing the unified_feed schema) — also update the hand-written sections at the top of this file. Those won't autogen.
5. **Never commit a real source name.** `tools/check_anonymization.py` blocks PRs that leak one. The blocklist is hashed at `.anonymization-blocklist.sha256` so you can't reverse it from the repo.

See `AGENTS.md` for the convention entry-point that may have additional pointers depending on which tool you're using.
